title: narcissus × albidus subsp. foliosus
intro: Narcissus × albidus subsp. foliosus, also known as the white narcissus or wild daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the narcissus family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and aiding in digestion, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Narcissus × albidus subsp. foliosus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
